name: @mx-loom/mcp
description: mx-loom generated MCP server (T109). Turns the canonical `@mx-loom/registry` descriptor set into a live Model Context Protocol endpoint — the universal binding for MCP-capable target runtimes (Claude/ADK/OpenCode/custom; Pi uses native registration per T204). Tools are generated by enumerating the registry (never hand-authored); the T102 result envelope (incl. `awaiting_approval`) maps onto an MCP `CallToolResult`; stdio + Streamable-HTTP transports. Secret-free by construction: every daemon call routes through the toolbelt `MxClient`/`MxSession`, so the env allowlist, credential-shaped-arg rejection, and inbound redaction stay in force, and enforcement stays out-of-process on the receiving daemon.
